description: ModuleId, FunctionId, RootId, CodeId all expose from_u32() or equivalent constructors, letting callers mint IDs outside the owning map. This bypasses the map's invariants (slot existence, reverse-lookup consistency). Each ID type should have only a private constructor; the owning map (ModuleMap, FunctionMap, RootMap, CodeMap) is the exclusive minting authority. Remove from_u32, any pub(crate) tuple constructors, and any test-only shims that create raw IDs. Callers that use from_u32 in tests need to go through the map's reference/define path instead.
title: fz-ehe.7: seal ID minting — only owning Maps can create ModuleId/FunctionId/RootId/CodeId; remove from_u32 and all external constructors
